name: 03_Text_Classification_NaiveBayes_20Newsgroups_ML
query: Por favor, implemente un clasificador Naive Bayes para el conjunto de datos 20 Newsgroups y guárdelo en un archivo llamado `src/model.py`. El conjunto de datos debe cargarse en `src/data_loader.py`. El programa debe manejar el preprocesamiento de datos, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales. Muestre la mejora de su clasificador generando nubes de palabras antes y después de entrenar su clasificador y guárdelas como `results/figures/wordcloud_before.png` y `results/figures/wordcloud_after.png`. Por favor, calcule e incluya características TF-IDF al cargar los datos en `src/data_loader.py`. Finalmente, imprima un informe de rendimiento (incluyendo precisión, recall y F1-score) y guárdelo como `results/metrics/performance.txt`. El modelo debe ser fácil de interpretar, y el informe final debe estar estructurado claramente para una revisión sencilla.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "20 Newsgroups" se utiliza en `src/data_loader.py`.

Se realiza el preprocesamiento de datos, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales. Las nubes de palabras se visualizan antes y después de entrenar el clasificador, y se guardan como `results/figures/wordcloud_before.png` y `results/figures/wordcloud_after.png`.
Se utilizan características "TF-IDF" al cargar los datos en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se implementa un "clasificador Naive Bayes" en `src/model.py`.
Se imprime un informe de rendimiento, incluyendo "precisión," "recall," y el "F1-score," y se guarda como `results/metrics/performance.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El modelo debe ser fácil de interpretar.
El informe final debe estar estructurado claramente para una revisión sencilla.

hint: In the query, there is a missing word "be" after the word "should" in "The dataset should loaded in `src/data_loader.py`".

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "20 Newsgroups" se utiliza en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `src/data_loader.py` does not implement the actual loading of the "20 Newsgroups" dataset. Although the string "20 Newsgroups" appears within a list of requirement criteria (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`) and a placeholder function (`requirement_notes`), the function `load_dataset()` is merely a placeholder that returns a dictionary with the string `"dataset": "loaded"`. There is no functional code, such as an import from `sklearn.datasets` or the use of `fetch_20newsgroups`, to actually utilize the dataset as required. Additionally, the trajectory evidence confirms that execution failed because the necessary libraries to load this dataset were not present or correctly implemented.

judge_stats.criteria: Se realiza el preprocesamiento de datos, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales. Las nubes de palabras se visualizan antes y después de entrenar el clasificador, y se guardan como `results/figures/wordcloud_before.png` y `results/figures/wordcloud_after.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The requirement is fully satisfied. The project structure confirms the existence of the files `results/figures/wordcloud_before.png` and `results/figures/wordcloud_after.png`. Additionally, the trajectory evidence explicitly states that the preprocessing steps (removal of stopwords, punctuation, and special characters) were completed successfully and that the system confirmed the creation and saving of both word cloud images in the specified paths without errors. Although the image content could not be rendered in the text-based evidence log, the file system metadata and execution logs verify their correct generation.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan características "TF-IDF" al cargar los datos en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not satisfied because the provided content of `src/data_loader.py` does not contain any implementation of "TF-IDF" features. The file only contains a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that returns a dictionary with a string reference to the requirements, but lacks the actual logic, imports (such as `TfidfVectorizer` from `sklearn`), or processing steps necessary to transform the "20 Newsgroups" dataset into TF-IDF features. Although the trajectory mentions a successful execution after fixing imports, the actual code provided in the file content is a "Minimal semantic implementation" that acts as a placeholder and does not perform the requested operation.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa un "clasificador Naive Bayes" en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `src/model.py` does not contain a functional implementation of a "Naive Bayes classifier". Upon inspection of the provided source code, the file only contains a placeholder function `build_model()` that returns the string `"model placeholder"`, along with metadata for benchmark judging. Although the trajectory evidence mentions previous attempts and execution errors related to a `NaiveBayesClassifier` class, the final version of the file in the workspace lacks the actual logic, classes, or mathematical implementation required for a Naive Bayes model.

judge_stats.criteria: Se imprime un informe de rendimiento, incluyendo "precisión," "recall," y el "F1-score," y se guarda como `results/metrics/performance.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not met because the content of the file `results/metrics/performance.txt` is merely a placeholder: `('placeholder\r\n', None)`. Although the trajectory evidence suggests that the evaluation script was executed and metrics were printed to the standard output (stdout), the actual file saved in the workspace does not contain the classification report (precision, recall, and F1-score) as required. The evidence shows the file exists in the project tree, but its content fails to reflect the actual performance metrics of the model.
